text.title: Content Audit Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Content Audit. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Audit covers every URL or asset, not a sample; a sample-based audit cannot guarantee orphan-free results.
rule.rationale: Sample audits miss orphan or stale content that hurts trust and SEO.

rule.statement: Each row carries owner, last-updated date, and a fresh-status flag (fresh / stale / orphan).
rule.rationale: Without an owner and freshness flag, cleanup decisions stall.

rule.statement: Each row gets a remove / update / keep decision driven by a documented matrix (traffic × value × freshness).
rule.rationale: Decisions without a matrix slip back to taste.

rule.statement: Lighthouse and accessibility scan results are joined into the audit so SEO and a11y debt are visible per row.
rule.rationale: Audits without these flags leave systemic debt invisible.

rule.statement: Audit ends with an action list, not a spreadsheet; every action has an owner and a target sprint.
rule.rationale: Spreadsheet-only outputs rarely turn into work.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-content-audit.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
